text: That was the first time at a company that's like exclusively cyber.

And so one of the things that was similar from crowd to here, right, and I mean, since you're working with other clients in cyber, you're probably seeing it there as well, it's like the good news in cyber is like you.
We were, like, every other space I've ever been in tech, you were, like, running around the company desperately trying to find somebody to create content for you, right?
You were desperately trying to turn, you know, company leaders into thought leaders, right?
You're trying to turn, you know, like, usually the best kind of domain experts you have tend to be lower in the totem pole.
And so now you're trying to bring them up and have them be spokespeople or content creators or whatever, right?
And so cyber is kind of the opposite, right, where it's, like, you're just, you're blessed with an army of genuine thought leaders, and many of which who want to contribute to the blog.
So it's actually the opposite experience where it's, like, you're often playing, you know, kind of editorial board and traffic cop over here.
Like, you know, like other B2B companies where you're, like, desperate, you know, you're begging people.

so when we, and even labs will do that, when labs, the research team writes the blogs, their blogs, they will go to our detections engineering team and be like, hey, can we get the IOCs that match up with this research so that it's not like I'm just learning about this bad guy or this malware or this vulnerability, but I'm actually able to then take that information and effectively use it in defense of my environment.
